content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 30, 1991

Isolina Ricci, Ph.D.
Administrative Office of the Courts
Judicial Council of California
300 2nd Street, South Tower
San Francisco, CA 94107

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-91-207

Dear Dr. Ricci:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance
provided to you under the conflict of interest disclosure
provisions, and the gift and honoraria provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1/

Your letter accurately states the advice that was provided to
you concerning the disclosure of sources of income to the New
Family Center, a business entity of which you are the sole
proprietor.

You indicated that you disclose the business entity as an
investment on Schedule A of your statement of economic interests
(Form 730), and disclose your pro rata share (100%) of the total
income received by the business entity on Schedule D. (The
business entity is the "source" of the income disclosed on
Schedule D.)

Sources of income to the business entity are required to be
disclosed on Schedule H-2 of your statement of economic interests
if the business entity receives $10,000 or more during a calendar
year from a single source, if the source is of the type that is
described in your disclosure category under the conflict of

_______________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Isolina Ricci
Page Two

interest code of the Judicial Council of California.  (Section
87207.)

The honoraria limitation of Section 89503 does not apply to:

   Earned income for personal services which are customarily
   provided in connection with the practice of a bona fide
   business, trade, or profession, such as teaching, practicing
   law, medicine, insurance, real estate, banking, or building
   contracting, unless the sole or predominant activity of the
   business, trade, or profession is making speeches.

                                       (Section 89502(c).)

   For your information, at its meeting in May, the Commission
will consider regulations to define the terms used in the Act's
new gift and honoraria provisions.  Proposed Regulation 18953 will
define the term "bona fide business or trade" for purposes of the
honoraria limitations.  We have added your name to the mailing
list to receive copies of the proposed regulations.

   If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                       Sincerely,

                                       Scott H. Hallabrin
                                       Acting General Counsel

                                       [signature]

                                       By: Jeanne Pritchard
                                           Chief, Technical Assistance
                                           and Analysis Division

sections.question_synthetic: Dr. Ricci requested confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper disclosure of income sources from her business entity (New Family Center) on her statement of economic interests, and whether honoraria limitations apply to earned income from her business activities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that Dr. Ricci should disclose the business entity on Schedule A and her pro rata share of income on Schedule D, with sources of income to the business entity required on Schedule H-2 if a single source provides $10,000 or more annually. The honoraria limitation does not apply to earned income for personal services customarily provided in connection with a bona fide business, trade, or profession.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains confirmation of prior telephone advice rather than response to formal written question. No distinct QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but substantive advice is clearly provided. References to specific Government Code sections and regulations included. Signature block shows Scott H. Hallabrin with Jeanne Pritchard as Chief of Technical Assistance and Analysis Division.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Dr. Ricci requested confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper disclosure of income sources from her business entity (New Family Center) on her statement of economic interests, and whether honoraria limitations apply to earned income from her business activities.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirmed that Dr. Ricci should disclose the business entity on Schedule A and her pro rata share of income on Schedule D, with sources of income to the business entity required on Schedule H-2 if a single source provides $10,000 or more annually. The honoraria limitation does not apply to earned income for personal services customarily provided in connection with a bona fide business, trade, or profession.

You indicated that you disclose the business entity as an investment on Schedule A of your statement of economic interests (Form 730), and disclose your pro rata share (100%) of the total income received by the business entity on Schedule D. (The business entity is the "source" of the income disclosed on Schedule D.) Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Isolina Ricci Page Two interest code of the Judicial Council of California. (Section 87207.) (Section 89502(c).) For your information, at its meeting in May, the Commission will consider regulations to define the terms used in the Act's new gift and honoraria provisions. embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms advice provided to Dr. Ricci regarding proper disclosure of income from her sole proprietorship (New Family Center) on her statement of economic interests and clarifies that honoraria limitations do not apply to earned income from customary business activities.
